insights.claim: ZIPs in the most-deprived tenth (by ADI) average 7.5 points higher than the least-deprived tenth.
insights.method_note: Population-weighted decile means over Area Deprivation Index.

insights.claim: Across ZIPs, frequent poor mental health is most associated with area deprivation index (Spearman ρ=0.715, n=31,222).
insights.method_note: Spearman rank correlation, ecological (ZIP-level); not causal.
Maximum ZIP value (ties broken by population).

insights.claim: About 23.0 million people live in ZIPs above the high-burden threshold (19.3%).
insights.method_note: Sum of population in ZIPs at or above the 90th percentile (19.3%).
